title: Java Object-Oriented Programming
description: Practice classes, objects, inheritance, and polymorphism in Java

questions.question: What is an object in Java?
questions.explanation: An object is an instance created from a class.

questions.question: Which keyword is used for inheritance in Java?
questions.explanation: extends is used when one class inherits from another class.

questions.question: Which keyword is used to implement an interface?
questions.explanation: implements connects a class to an interface.

questions.question: What is encapsulation?
options: Hiding internal data and exposing methods
questions.explanation: Encapsulation bundles data and methods while restricting direct access.

questions.question: Which access modifier makes a member visible only within the same class?
questions.explanation: private members are accessible only inside the class.

questions.question: What is method overloading?
options: Using the same method name with different parameters
Overriding a method in a subclass
Calling a method from another class
questions.explanation: Overloading means same name, different parameter lists.

questions.question: What is method overriding?
options: Replacing a superclass method in a subclass
questions.explanation: Overriding lets a subclass provide a new implementation for a parent method.

questions.question: Which keyword refers to the current object?
questions.explanation: this refers to the current instance of the class.

questions.question: Which keyword calls the parent class constructor?
questions.explanation: super() is used to call the superclass constructor.

questions.question: What is polymorphism?
options: One class with only one method
A method with no return value
questions.explanation: Polymorphism allows a single interface to represent different underlying forms.
